developer_prompt: Map the minimal cloud render architecture: web app, API, DB-backed RenderJob queue, R2 storage, and Railway render-worker. Keep v1 KISS: no Redis, no WebSockets, worker concurrency 1.
Add apps/render-worker to the existing pnpm monorepo and document how apps/web, apps/api, and apps/render-worker share packages without circular dependencies.
Create root scripts to run web, api, and render-worker together on localhost using pnpm/turbo, with clear ports and env examples for Windows/WSL.
Plan Railway deployment with three services from the same repo: web, api, render-worker. Specify service start commands, root paths, and env variables.
Create a single env contract for local and Railway: DATABASE_URL, R2_* variables, API_PUBLIC_URL, API_INTERNAL_URL, RENDER_WORKER_CONCURRENCY, and render limits.
Add Prisma models Render and RenderJob with statuses, userId, projectId, storage keys, phase, progress, attempts, lockedAt, lockedBy, errorCode, and timestamps.
Generate and validate the Prisma migration for Render/RenderJob. Keep relationships explicit: User -> Render, Project -> Render, Render -> RenderJob.
Define minimal RenderStatus and RenderJobStatus enums: QUEUED, PROCESSING, READY/SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELLED, DELETED where appropriate.
Create packages/render-contracts with CloudRenderPayloadV1. Include version, renderId, projectId, asset IDs/keys, outputKey, preset, duration, dimensions, fps, and subtitle settings.
Define R2 storage key conventions for inputs, subtitles, final MP4, and poster JPG. Worker must never accept arbitrary client-provided keys without DB validation.
Implement POST /api/generate as a fail-fast job creator only. It must validate auth, ownership, limits, project assets, and create Render + RenderJob in one transaction.
Add server-side validation for /api/generate payload: projectId, preset, outputFormat, optional range, and reject unknown fields or unsupported presets.
Ensure /api/generate requires an authenticated user. No guest cloud render in v1.
In /api/generate, verify the project belongs to the session user and all referenced assets belong to that project/user.
Add conservative quota checks before job creation: one active render per user, max queued renders per user, max daily renders, max duration, max resolution, max fps.
Return clear typed errors from /api/generate: AUTH_REQUIRED, QUOTA_EXCEEDED, PROJECT_NOT_FOUND, ASSET_MISSING, LIMIT_EXCEEDED, UNSUPPORTED_PRESET.
Create Render and RenderJob in a single DB transaction. Render.userId/projectId are source of truth; RenderJob references renderId only.
Implement GET /api/renders/:id. It must require auth and return only renders owned by the session user.
Implement GET /api/projects/:projectId/renders for a project's render history. Enforce ownership and return latest renders first.
Implement POST /api/renders/:id/download-url. Only READY renders get a short-lived signed R2 download URL.
Implement POST /api/renders/:id/cancel. QUEUED jobs become CANCELLED; PROCESSING jobs set cancelRequested for the worker.
Implement DELETE /api/renders/:id. Soft-delete the Render and optionally schedule R2 cleanup. Do not physically delete during the request if risky.
Use frontend polling for v1 render status every 2–5 seconds. Do not add WebSockets yet.
Add an optional lightweight SSE endpoint /api/renders/:id/events only after polling works. It should stream Render status/phase/progress updates and reuse the same response shape.
If implementing SSE, add auth/ownership checks at connection time, heartbeat comments, connection cleanup, and fallback to polling.
Create apps/render-worker Node entrypoint. On boot, load env, connect DB/R2, generate workerId, cleanup stale temp dirs, and start a polling loop.
Implement worker polling with a 2–5 second idle delay. Avoid tight DB loops.
Implement atomic job claim: only one worker can transition a QUEUED RenderJob to PROCESSING using a transaction or conditional update.
After claiming, worker reloads Render, Project, and Assets from DB and verifies user/project ownership before downloading anything.
Parse CloudRenderPayloadV1 with strict schema validation. Fail jobs with BAD_PAYLOAD if version/required fields are invalid.
Add stale job recovery: PROCESSING jobs locked too long are requeued if attempts remain, otherwise FAILED_STALE.
Implement maxAttempts=2. Retry only infrastructure failures; do not retry bad input, unsupported media, quota/limit failures, or user cancellation.
Create isolated temp workspace /tmp/renders/{jobId}. Download inputs there, write output there, and delete in finally.
Create shared R2 helper to download DB-approved objects to local temp files with size/error handling.
Use ffprobe to validate input video/audio streams, dimensions, duration, and basic codec availability before rendering.
Build a small typed FFmpeg command builder for v1: input video + input audio + SRT subtitles -> H.264/AAC MP4.
Implement subtitle burn-in using FFmpeg subtitles/ass filter. Start with simple SRT styling and document limitations versus canvas preview.
Map app subtitle settings to FFmpeg/ASS style fields where feasible: font, size, color, outline, alignment, margins. Keep unsupported styles explicit.
Run FFmpeg with -progress pipe:1 -nostats and parse out_time_ms to update RenderJob progress during RENDERING.
Use phase-weighted progress: QUEUED 0, DOWNLOADING 5-15, PROBING 15-20, RENDERING 20-85, UPLOADING 85-95, FINALIZING 95-100, READY 100.
Add hard job timeout: min(duration * 5, 20 minutes) for v1. Kill FFmpeg on timeout and mark FAILED_TIMEOUT.
During FFmpeg, poll cancelRequested every few seconds. Kill FFmpeg gracefully, then force kill if needed, and mark CANCELLED.
After FFmpeg finishes, verify output.mp4 exists, size > 0, and ffprobe sees video/audio streams with expected duration.
Generate poster.jpg from midpoint or first valid frame after output validation. Upload poster to R2 with the render.
Upload final.mp4 and poster.jpg to R2 with correct content types. Do not keep final files on Railway disk.
After upload succeeds, update Render READY with outputKey, posterKey, duration, size, completedAt; update RenderJob SUCCEEDED.
On any worker error, save errorCode and safe user-facing errorMessage. Include internal details only in server logs.
Enforce worker-side limits again: max duration, max dimensions, max fps, max input size. Never trust API-only validation.
Set RENDER_WORKER_CONCURRENCY=1 for v1. Design loop so increasing concurrency later is a config change, not a rewrite.
Add apps/render-worker/Dockerfile using node:22-bookworm with ffmpeg, fontconfig, and safe fonts installed.
Document Railway env vars for worker and api. Worker should have no public domain and no user-facing routes.
Write local Windows/WSL setup notes: install ffmpeg locally or use Docker, configure R2 dev bucket, run pnpm dev, verify worker can render a sample.
Add a small local render fixture: short video, short audio, sample SRT. Use it to test worker end-to-end without the full UI.
Add tests for /api/generate: unauthenticated, wrong owner, missing asset, quota exceeded, valid job creation.
Add unit tests for payload parsing, command building, phase progress mapping, and error classification.
Add a local smoke script that inserts a RenderJob, runs the worker once, and verifies final.mp4 is uploaded or written to a test output.
Add a 'Save Online / Cloud Render' button near export controls. Keep local Draft export separate.
Reuse existing save/export modal for cloud render. Add cloud option, preset selector, expected duration/limits, and auth-required messaging.
Wire the cloud render button to POST /api/generate and show immediate QUEUED state on success.
Poll GET /api/renders/:id while queued/processing. Show phase, percentage, and safe error messages.
If SSE is added, make the modal use SSE when available and polling fallback otherwise. Do not block v1 on SSE.
Add a simple render history list under project/export panel: status, preset, createdAt, download button when READY.
When render is READY, request signed download URL and open/download it. Do not expose raw R2 keys in the UI.
Show user-friendly limits before creating a job: max duration, max resolution, active render count. Keep API as source of truth.
Add minimal admin/debug render jobs view only if already have admin role. Show stuck jobs, attempts, phase, progress, error.
Add structured logs with renderId/jobId/workerId/userId for API creation and worker processing. Avoid logging signed URLs or secrets.
Review every path for abuse: no anonymous render, no arbitrary storage keys, one active job/user, hard timeouts, cleanup in finally.
Remove anything not needed for POC: no Redis, no WebSockets requirement, no multi-worker autoscale, no full canvas layer render in worker.
Write docs/render-worker.md explaining local dev, Railway deployment, env vars, job lifecycle, limits, and known v1 limitations.
Define POC acceptance: logged-in user can click Save Online, API creates job, worker renders MP4 with subtitles, uploads to R2, UI shows READY and downloads.
Add TODO notes for v2 only: Redis/BullMQ, SSE-only progress, full project render, MP4 variants, paid quotas, cloud render retries/dashboard.
